heading: Pipeline safety information grants to communities
label: 60130 Pipeline safety information grants to communities

children.heading: .—In general
children.label: 1 .—In general
children.text: The Secretary of Transportation may make grants for technical assistance to local communities, Indian Tribes, and groups of individuals (not including for-profit entities) relating to the safety of pipeline facilities in local communities, other than facilities regulated under ( et seq.). No grants may be awarded under section 60114(g) until the Secretary has established competitive procedures for awarding grants under this section and criteria for selecting grant recipients. Except as provided in subsection (c)(2), the amount of any grant under this section may not exceed $100,000 for a single grant recipient. The Secretary shall establish appropriate procedures to ensure the proper use of funds provided under this section.Public Law 93–15343 U.S.C. 1651

children.heading: .—Demonstration grants
children.label: 2 .—Demonstration grants
children.text: At least the first 3 grants awarded under this section shall be demonstration grants for the purpose of demonstrating and evaluating the utility of grants under this section. Each such demonstration grant shall not exceed $25,000.

children.label: A
children.text: the technical findings made possible by the grants are made available to the relevant operators; and

children.label: B
children.text: open communication between the grant recipients, local operators, local communities, and other interested parties is encouraged.

children.heading: .—Prohibited Uses
children.label: b .—Prohibited Uses
children.text: Funds provided under this section to grant recipients and their contractors may not be used for lobbying, for direct advocacy for or against a pipeline construction or expansion project, or in direct support of litigation.

children.heading: .—In general
children.label: 1 .—In general
children.text: Subject to paragraph (2), out of amounts made available under section 2(b) of the PIPES Act of 2016 (; ), the Secretary shall use $2,000,000 for each of fiscal years 2021 through 2023 to carry out this section.Public Law 114–183130 Stat. 515

children.heading: .—Improving technical assistance
children.label: 2 .—Improving technical assistance
children.text: From the amounts used to carry out this section under paragraph (1) each fiscal year, the Secretary shall award $1,000,000 to an eligible applicant through a competitive selection process for the purpose of improving the quality of technical assistance provided to communities or individuals under this section.

children.heading: .—Limitation
children.label: 3 .—Limitation
children.text: Any amounts used to carry out this section shall not be derived from user fees collected under section 60301.

children.heading: .—Technical assistance
children.label: 1 .—Technical assistance
children.text: The term “technical assistance” means engineering, research, and other scientific analysis of pipeline safety issues, including the promotion of public participation on technical pipeline safety issues in proceedings related to this chapter.

children.label: C
children.text: is able to provide individuals and communities with technical assistance; and

children.label: D
children.text: was established with funds designated for the purpose of community service through the implementation of relating to violations of this chapter.section 3553 of title 18
